speaker: LEONTES
text: His cupbearer—whom I from meaner form

text: Have benched and reared to worship, who mayst see

speaker: LEONTES
text: Plainly as heaven sees Earth and Earth sees heaven

speaker: LEONTES

speaker: LEONTES
text: To give mine enemy a lasting wink,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Which draft to me were cordial.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Sir, my lord,

speaker: CAMILLO
text: I could do this, and that with no rash potion,

speaker: CAMILLO
text: But with a ling’ring dram that should not work

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Maliciously like poison. But I cannot

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Believe this crack to be in my dread mistress,

speaker: CAMILLO
text: So sovereignly being honorable. I have loved thee—

speaker: LEONTES
text: Make that thy question, and go rot!

speaker: LEONTES
text: Dost think I am so muddy, so unsettled,

speaker: LEONTES
text: To appoint myself in this vexation, sully

speaker: LEONTES
text: The purity and whiteness of my sheets—

speaker: LEONTES
text: Which to preserve is sleep, which being spotted

speaker: LEONTES
text: Is goads, thorns, nettles, tails of wasps—

speaker: LEONTES
text: Give scandal to the blood o’ th’ Prince, my son,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Who I do think is mine and love as mine,

speaker: LEONTES
text: Without ripe moving to ’t? Would I do this?

speaker: LEONTES
text: Could man so blench?

speaker: CAMILLO
text: I must believe you, sir.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: I do, and will fetch off Bohemia for ’t—

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Provided that, when he’s removed, your Highness

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Will take again your queen as yours at first,

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Even for your son’s sake, and thereby for sealing

speaker: CAMILLO
text: The injury of tongues in courts and kingdoms

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Known and allied to yours.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Thou dost advise me

speaker: LEONTES
text: Even so as I mine own course have set down.

speaker: LEONTES
text: I’ll give no blemish to her honor, none.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: My lord,

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Go then, and with a countenance as clear

speaker: CAMILLO
text: As friendship wears at feasts, keep with Bohemia

speaker: CAMILLO
text: And with your queen. I am his cupbearer.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: If from me he have wholesome beverage,

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Account me not your servant.

speaker: LEONTES
text: This is all.

speaker: LEONTES
text: Do ’t and thou hast the one half of my heart;

speaker: LEONTES
text: Do ’t not, thou splitt’st thine own.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: I’ll do ’t, my lord.

speaker: LEONTES
text: I will seem friendly, as thou hast advised me.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: O miserable lady! But, for me,

speaker: CAMILLO
text: What case stand I in? I must be the poisoner

text: Of good Polixenes, and my ground to do ’t

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Is the obedience to a master, one

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Who in rebellion with himself will have

speaker: CAMILLO
text: All that are his so too. To do this deed,

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Promotion follows. If I could find example

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Of thousands that had struck anointed kings

speaker: CAMILLO
text: And flourished after, I’d not do ’t. But since

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Nor brass, nor stone, nor parchment bears not one,

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Let villainy itself forswear ’t. I must

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Forsake the court. To do ’t or no is certain

speaker: CAMILLO
text: To me a breakneck. Happy star reign now!

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Here comes Bohemia.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: This is strange. Methinks

text: My favor here begins to warp. Not speak?—

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Good day, Camillo.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Hail, most royal sir.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: What is the news i’ th’ court?

speaker: CAMILLO
text: None rare, my lord.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: The King hath on him such a countenance

speaker: POLIXENES
text: As he had lost some province and a region

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Loved as he loves himself. Even now I met him

speaker: POLIXENES
text: With customary compliment, when he,

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Wafting his eyes to th’ contrary and falling

speaker: POLIXENES
text: A lip of much contempt, speeds from me, and

speaker: POLIXENES
text: So leaves me to consider what is breeding

speaker: POLIXENES
text: That changes thus his manners.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: I dare not know, my lord.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: How, dare not? Do not? Do you know and dare not?

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Be intelligent to me—’tis thereabouts;

speaker: POLIXENES
text: For to yourself what you do know, you must,

speaker: POLIXENES
text: And cannot say you dare not. Good Camillo,

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Your changed complexions are to me a mirror

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Which shows me mine changed too, for I must be

speaker: POLIXENES
text: A party in this alteration, finding

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Myself thus altered with ’t.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: There is a sickness

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Which puts some of us in distemper, but

speaker: CAMILLO
text: I cannot name the disease, and it is caught

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Of you that yet are well.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: How caught of me?

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Make me not sighted like the basilisk.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: I have looked on thousands who have sped the better

speaker: POLIXENES
text: By my regard, but killed none so. Camillo,

speaker: POLIXENES
text: As you are certainly a gentleman, thereto

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Clerklike experienced, which no less adorns

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Our gentry than our parents’ noble names,

speaker: POLIXENES
text: In whose success we are gentle, I beseech you,

speaker: POLIXENES
text: If you know aught which does behoove my knowledge

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Thereof to be informed, imprison ’t not

speaker: POLIXENES
text: In ignorant concealment.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: I may not answer.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: A sickness caught of me, and yet I well?

speaker: POLIXENES
text: I must be answered. Dost thou hear, Camillo?

speaker: POLIXENES
text: I conjure thee by all the parts of man

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Which honor does acknowledge, whereof the least

speaker: POLIXENES
text: Is not this suit of mine, that thou declare

text: Is creeping toward me; how far off, how near;

text: Which way to be prevented, if to be;

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Sir, I will tell you,

text: Since I am charged in honor and by him

text: That I think honorable. Therefore mark my counsel,

text: I mean to utter it, or both yourself and me

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Cry lost, and so goodnight.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: On, good Camillo.

speaker: CAMILLO
text: I am appointed him to murder you.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: By whom, Camillo?

speaker: CAMILLO
text: By the King.

speaker: POLIXENES
text: For what?

speaker: CAMILLO
text: He thinks, nay with all confidence he swears,

speaker: CAMILLO
text: As he had seen ’t or been an instrument

speaker: CAMILLO
text: To vice you to ’t, that you have touched his queen

speaker: CAMILLO
text: Forbiddenly.

text: O, then my best blood turn

speaker: POLIXENES
text: To an infected jelly, and my name

text: Be yoked with his that did betray the Best!

text: Turn then my freshest reputation to

text: A savor that may strike the dullest nostril

text: Where I arrive, and my approach be shunned,

text: Nay, hated too, worse than the great’st infection

text: That e’er was heard or read.

text: Is piled upon his faith and will continue

text: If therefore you dare trust my honesty,

speaker: CAMILLO
text: That lies enclosèd in this trunk which you
